fields.name: Bajang
fields.description: A remote and peaceful national park in far-western Nepal, known for its rolling green meadows, rich biodiversity, and spiritual significance as the meditation site of Khaptad Baba.

fields.name: Ilam
fields.description: A beautiful hill town in eastern Nepal, famous for its lush tea gardens, rolling hills, sunrise views, and cultural blend of Limbu traditions.

fields.name: Janakpur
fields.description: The cultural and religious capital of the Mithila region, revered as the birthplace of Goddess Sita. Known for the grand Janaki Temple and vibrant festivals like Bibaha Panchami.

fields.name: Dolakha
fields.description: A historic hill town offering panoramic views of the Himalayas, traditional Newari architecture, and the famous Rani Mahal (often called the Taj Mahal of Nepal).

fields.name: Bardiya
fields.description: One of Nepal’s largest and most pristine national parks, home to endangered species like Bengal tigers, one-horned rhinos, and wild elephants. Popular for jungle safaris.

fields.name: Sunsari
fields.description: Located in southeastern Nepal, this protected wetland is a bird-watcher’s paradise, attracting migratory birds from Siberia and Tibet. Also known for rare water buffaloes (Arna).

fields.name: Taplejung
fields.description: A sacred pilgrimage site in eastern Nepal, perched high in the Himalayas, offering spiritual experiences along with breathtaking mountain views of Kanchenjunga.

fields.name: Kaski
fields.description: Kaski District, located in Gandaki Province, is one of Nepal’s most popular tourist destinations, home to the scenic city of Pokhara. It is famous for its natural beauty, including Phewa Lake, Begnas Lake, Devi’s Fall, and the Seti River Gorge. The district offers breathtaking views of the Annapurna and Machhapuchhre (Fishtail) mountains

fields.name: Ilam
fields.description: Tea Gardens & Hillside Town in Ilam, Nepal, are the district’s highlights—lush green tea estates spread across rolling hills, with serene hillside towns like Kanyam, Antu, and Fikkal. They are known for breathtaking views, cool climate, sunrise points, and a peaceful natural environment, making Ilam a popular eco-tourism destination.

fields.name: Dolakha
fields.description: Dolakha is a historic hill town in Bagmati Province, renowned for the sacred Dolakha Bhimsen Temple, its Newar settlements and traditional architecture, cultural festivals, and scenic views of Gaurishankar Himal and the Rolwaling Valley.

fields.name: Bajhang
fields.description: It is famous for its rolling grasslands, forests, and Khaptad Baba Ashram. The park is rich in biodiversity with medicinal herbs, wildlife, and over 200 bird species, offering peaceful natural beauty and cultural significance.

fields.name: Janakpur
fields.description: Janakpur is a historic city in Madhesh Province, Nepal, known as the birthplace of Goddess Sita and the capital of the ancient Mithila Kingdom.It is a major Hindu pilgrimage site, famous for the Janaki Mandir, beautiful Mithila art, and vibrant festivals like Vivah Panchami.

fields.name: Taplejung
fields.description: Pathivara Devi Temple is a sacred Hindu shrine located in Taplejung District, Koshi Province, Nepal.It is one of the most important Shakti Peeths, where devotees worship Goddess Pathivara for blessings of wealth, health, and protection. Situated at about 3,794 meters, the temple offers spiritual significance along with stunning views of the Himalayas, attracting both pilgrims and trekkers.

fields.name: Sunsari
fields.description: Koshi Tappu Wildlife Reserve is located in Koshi Province, eastern Nepal, along the floodplains of the Koshi River.It is famous for being a Ramsar site and a haven for birdwatchers, with over 500 bird species. The reserve is also home to the rare wild water buffalo (Arna), as well as deer, dolphins, and diverse aquatic life.

fields.name: Bardiya
fields.description: It is the largest national park in the Terai, famous for its Bengal tigers, one-horned rhinoceroses, elephants, gharials, and rich birdlife. With dense forests, grasslands, and the Karnali River, it offers a true wilderness experience and is one of Nepal’s best spots for tiger sightings and jungle safaris.

fields.name: Kaski
fields.description: Kaski District, located in Gandaki Province, is one of Nepal’s most popular tourist destinations, home to the scenic city of Pokhara. It is famous for its natural beauty, including Phewa Lake, Begnas Lake, Devi’s Fall, and the Seti River Gorge. The district offers breathtaking views of the Annapurna and Machhapuchhre (Fishtail) mountains, making it a hub for trekking routes such as Annapurna Base Camp, Mardi Himal, and Ghorepani Poon Hill. Rich in cultural diversity, Kaski also showcases Gurung, Magar, and Newar heritage, with villages like Ghandruk and Dhampus attracting visitors for their traditional architecture and warm hospitality.

fields.title: Pokhara: City of Lakes & HimalayasUntitled
fields.overview: Pokhara, Nepal’s most scenic city, is surrounded by serene lakes and the Annapurna Himalayas. Known for adventure sports and breathtaking views, it’s the perfect blend of relaxation and adrenaline.
fields.highlight:
Phewa Lake & Tal Barahi Temple

World Peace Pagoda

Davis Falls & Gupteshwor Cave

Sarangkot Sunrise Viewpoint
fields.itinerary:
Day 1: Arrival in Pokhara, evening boat ride on Phewa Lake

Day 2: Sunrise at Sarangkot, visit World Peace Pagoda & caves

Day 3: Adventure activities (paragliding/zip line/ultralight flight)

Day 4: Shopping at Lakeside & departure
fields.cost_details:
🚌 Transport (Kathmandu–Pokhara round trip): $20 – $50
🏨 Accommodation: $25 per night × 3 nights = $75
🍲 Food: $15 – $20/day × 4 days = $60 – $80
🎟️ Entrance Fees & Activities: $50 – $120
🛍️ Other Expenses: $30 – $50
Total: $235 – $375

fields.title: Chitwan National Park: Jungle Safari AdventureUntitled
fields.overview: Experience Nepal’s wildlife in Chitwan, home to one-horned rhinos, Bengal tigers, and elephants. A UNESCO World Heritage Site offering jungle safaris and Tharu cultural programs.
fields.highlight:
Jeep Safari & Canoeing in Rapti River

Bird Watching & Elephant Breeding Center

Tharu Culture Dance Show
fields.itinerary:
Day 1: Drive to Chitwan, evening cultural program

Day 2: Jeep Safari, canoe ride & jungle walk

Day 3: Bird watching, village tour, return to Kathmandu
fields.cost_details:
🚌 Transport (Kathmandu–Chitwan round trip): $15 – $25
🏨 Accommodation: $20 per night × 2 nights = $40
🍲 Food: $15/day × 3 days = $45
🎟️ Safari Permits & Park Fees: $50 – $100
🛍️ Other Expenses: $20 – $30
Total: $170 – $240

fields.title: Lumbini: Birthplace of Lord BuddhaUntitled
fields.overview: Lumbini, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is the birthplace of Lord Buddha. Explore peaceful monasteries built by nations worldwide and the sacred Maya Devi Temple.
fields.highlight:
Maya Devi Temple

Ashokan Pillar

World Peace Pagoda

International Monasteries
fields.itinerary:
Day 1: Drive/Flight to Lumbini, evening monastery walk

Day 2: Visit Maya Devi Temple & Ashokan Pillar

Day 3: Explore monasteries & World Peace Pagoda, departure
fields.cost_details:
🚌 Transport (Kathmandu–Lumbini round trip): $20 – $40 (bus) / $150 – $200 (flight)
🏨 Accommodation: $20 per night × 2 nights = $40
🍲 Food: $15/day × 3 days = $45
🎟️ Entrance Fees: $20 – $30
🛍️ Other Expenses: $20 – $30
Total: $145 – $340

fields.title: Bandipur: Hilltop Heritage VillageUntitled
fields.overview: Bandipur is a charming hilltop town with preserved Newari culture, traditional houses, and stunning views of the Himalayas.
fields.highlight:
Siddha Cave (largest cave in Nepal)

Tundikhel Viewpoint

Newari architecture & culture

Hiking around Bandipur
fields.itinerary:
Day 1: Drive to Bandipur, explore village & sunset view

Day 2: Visit Siddha Cave & surrounding hills

Day 3: Cultural walk, return to Kathmandu
fields.cost_details:
🚌 Transport (Kathmandu–Bandipur round trip): $10 – $20
🏨 Accommodation: $25 per night × 2 nights = $50
🍲 Food: $15/day × 3 days = $45
🎟️ Entrance & Activities: $10 – $20
🛍️ Other Expenses: $20 – $30
Total: $135 – $165

fields.title: Nagarkot & Bhaktapur: Sunrise & HeritageUntitled
fields.overview: Nagarkot is famous for sunrise views over the Himalayas, while Bhaktapur offers ancient temples and cultural heritage.
fields.highlight:
Nagarkot Sunrise Viewpoint

Bhaktapur Durbar Square (UNESCO site)

Nyatapola Temple

Pottery Square
fields.itinerary:
Day 1: Drive to Bhaktapur, explore Durbar Square & temples

Day 2: Overnight in Nagarkot, sunrise & mountain views

Day 3: Return to Kathmandu
fields.cost_details:
🚌 Transport (Kathmandu–Bhaktapur–Nagarkot): $10 – $15
🏨 Accommodation: $20 per night × 2 nights = $40
🍲 Food: $15/day × 3 days = $45
🎟️ Entrance Fees: $15 – $20
🛍️ Other Expenses: $20 – $30
Total: $130 – $150

fields.title: Ghandruk Village: Gateway to AnnapurnaUntitled
fields.overview: Ghandruk is a traditional Gurung village with panoramic views of Annapurna and Machhapuchhre. A cultural and scenic gem.
fields.highlight:
Gurung Museum & Culture

Annapurna & Machhapuchhre Views

Local homestays & organic food
fields.itinerary:
Day 1: Drive to Nayapul, trek to Ghandruk

Day 2: Explore Ghandruk village, Gurung Museum

Day 3: Sunrise view, trek back to Nayapul, return to Pokhara
fields.cost_details:
🚌 Transport (Pokhara–Nayapul round trip): $15 – $20
🏨 Accommodation: $15 per night × 2 nights = $30
🍲 Food: $15/day × 3 days = $45
🚶 Trekking Permits (ACAP, TIMS): $30 – $40
🛍️ Other Expenses: $20 – $30
Total: $140 – $165

fields.title: Rara Lake: Hidden Jewel of the HimalayasUntitled
fields.overview: Rara Lake, Nepal’s largest lake, lies in the remote Karnali region. Surrounded by Rara National Park, it offers serene beauty, blue waters, and Himalayan wilderness.
fields.highlight:
Rara Lake boating & hiking

Scenic mountain views

Rara National Park wildlife
fields.itinerary:
Day 1: Flight to Nepalgunj, overnight stay

Day 2: Flight to Talcha, trek to Rara Lake

Day 3: Full day exploration of Rara Lake

Day 4: Return trek to Talcha, flight back to Nepalgunj/Kathmandu
fields.cost_details:
✈️ Flights (Kathmandu–Nepalgunj–Talcha): $200 – $250
🏨 Accommodation: $20 per night × 3 nights = $60
🍲 Food: $15/day × 4 days = $60
🎟️ Park Fees: $20 – $30
🛍️ Other Expenses: $30 – $50
Total: $370 – $450

fields.title: Dolakha Jiri: Gateway to Everest
fields.overview: Jiri, often called the “Switzerland of Nepal,” is a historic gateway to the Everest region. Surrounded by lush hills, terraced farms, and traditional Sherpa villages, it offers cultural charm, mountain views, and a taste of rural Himalayan life.
fields.highlight:
Scenic drive through Dolakha & Jiri
Views of Gaurishankar Himal
Local Sherpa culture & monasteries
Organic farms & traditional food
fields.itinerary:
Day 1: Drive from Kathmandu to Dolakha, explore Dolakha Bhimsen Temple & overnight in Jiri
Day 2: Hike to local villages & viewpoints, experience Sherpa culture, stay overnight
Day 3: Explore surrounding hills, organic farms, return drive to Kathmandu
fields.cost_details:
🚌 Transport (Kathmandu–Dolakha–Jiri round trip): $15 – $20
🏠 Accommodation: $15 per night × 2 nights = $30
🍲 Food: $15/day × 3 days = $45
🎫 Entrance Fees/Monastery Donations: $10 – $15
👜 Other Expenses: $20 – $30
Total: $120 – $140

fields.title: Pathivara: Sacred Himalayan Pilgrimage
fields.overview: Pathivara Devi Temple, located in Taplejung District of Koshi Province, is one of Nepal’s most significant Hindu pilgrimage sites. Perched at 3,794 meters, it offers spiritual blessings along with breathtaking views of the Kanchenjunga range. The trek combines cultural devotion with natural beauty, passing through rhododendron forests, traditional Limbu villages, and rich biodiversity.
fields.highlight:
Pilgrimage to Pathivara Devi Temple (Shakti Peeth)
Panoramic views of Kanchenjunga & surrounding Himalayas
Trek through rhododendron forests & alpine meadows
Experience Limbu culture, traditions, and hospitality
Rich biodiversity with wildlife and birdlife en route
fields.itinerary:
Day 1: Fly or drive to Taplejung, overnight stay in Phungling Bazaar
Day 2: Trek from Phungling to Suketar/Deurali, stay overnight
Day 3: Early morning trek to Pathivara Temple, enjoy mountain views, return to Suketar, overnight stay
Day 4: Return trek to Taplejung, and fly/drive back
fields.cost_details:
🚌 Transport (Kathmandu–Taplejung round trip): $120 – $150
🏠 Accommodation: $20 per night × 3 nights = $60
🍲 Food: $15/day × 4 days = $60
🎫 Temple Donations/Entry Fees: $10 – $20
👜 Other Expenses (guides, porter, local transport): $40 – $60
Total: $290 – $350

fields.title: Jiri
fields.short_description: Jiri is the switzerland of Nepal.

fields.title: Ghandruk Village
fields.short_description: Ghandruk is a picturesque Gurung village in the Annapurna region of Gandaki Province, Nepal. It is famous for its stunning views of Annapurna SouthMountain). Known for its traditional stone houses, rich Gurung culture, and hospitality, the village is also a gateway for popular treks.

fields.title: Pathivara Temple
fields.short_description: Pathivara Devi Temple is one of the most important Hindu pilgrimage sites in Nepal, located at about 3,794 meters in Taplejung District, Koshi Province. Dedicated to Goddess Pathivara, it is a revered Shakti Peeth where devotees seek blessings of wealth, health, and protection.

fields.name: Prakriti
fields.message: Hello , Prakriti Khadka I am here to say you I love you to the moon and back .

fields.title: Everest Base Camp Trek
fields.description: The Everest Base Camp Trek is Nepal’s most iconic trekking adventure, taking you to the foot of the world’s highest mountain, Mt. Everest (8,848 m). The journey passes through Sherpa villages, suspension bridges, rhododendron forests, and high-altitude landscapes. Highlights include Namche Bazaar, Tengboche Monastery, and breathtaking views of Everest, Lhotse, Ama Dablam, and other Himalayan giants. Kala Patthar (5,545 m) offers the best panoramic sunrise views of Everest. This trek blends adventure with cultural immersion in the Khumbu region.
fields.itinerary:
Day 01: Arrival in Kathmandu (1,400 m)
Day 02: Flight to Lukla (2,800 m), trek to Phakding (2,610 m)
Day 03: Trek to Namche Bazaar (3,440 m)
Day 04: Acclimatization day at Namche Bazaar (hike to Everest View Hotel / Khumjung)
Day 05: Trek to Tengboche (3,870 m) – visit Tengboche Monastery
Day 06: Trek to Dingboche (4,410 m)
Day 07: Acclimatization day at Dingboche (hike to Nagarjun Hill / Chhukung)
Day 08: Trek to Lobuche (4,940 m)
Day 09: Trek to Gorakshep (5,164 m) & hike to Everest Base Camp (5,364 m), overnight at Gorakshep
Day 10: Hike to Kala Patthar (5,545 m) for sunrise, trek back to Pheriche (4,280 m)
Day 11: Trek to Namche Bazaar (3,440 m)
Day 12: Trek to Lukla (2,800 m)
Day 13: Flight to Kathmandu, free day for rest or sightseeing
Day 14: Departure from Kathmandu

fields.title: Annapurna Circuit Trek
fields.description:
The Annapurna Circuit Trek is one of the most famous treks in the world, 
renowned for its incredible diversity of landscapes and cultures. The trek encircles the 
Annapurna Massif, taking you from lush subtropical valleys and terraced farms to high alpine 
passes and arid landscapes resembling Tibet. The highlight is crossing Thorong La Pass (5,416 m), 
the highest point of the trek, with breathtaking views of Annapurna, Dhaulagiri, Machhapuchhre, 
and Manaslu. Along the route, trekkers experience Gurung, Manangi, and Thakali cultures, as well 
as visit sacred sites such as Muktinath Temple. This trek perfectly blends natural beauty with 
cultural richness.
fields.itinerary:
Day 01: Arrival in Kathmandu (1,400 m)
Day 02: Drive to Besisahar (760 m), trek to Bhulbhule
Day 03: Trek to Jagat (1,300 m)
Day 04: Trek to Dharapani (1,960 m)
Day 05: Trek to Chame (2,670 m)
Day 06: Trek to Pisang (3,200 m)
Day 07: Trek to Manang (3,540 m)
Day 08: Acclimatization day in Manang (hike to Gangapurna Lake or Ice Lake)
Day 09: Trek to Yak Kharka (4,050 m)
Day 10: Trek to Thorong Phedi (4,450 m)
Day 11: Cross Thorong La Pass (5,416 m), trek to Muktinath (3,760 m)
Day 12: Trek to Marpha (2,670 m) via Kagbeni and Jomsom
Day 13: Trek to Tatopani (1,200 m) – natural hot springs
Day 14: Trek to Ghorepani (2,850 m)
Day 15: Early morning hike to Poon Hill (3,210 m) for sunrise, trek to Nayapul, drive to Pokhara
Day 16: Drive or fly back to Kathmandu
Day 17: Free day in Kathmandu for sightseeing or rest
Day 18: Departure from Kathmandu

fields.title: Langtang Valley Trek
fields.description:
The Langtang Valley Trek is a beautiful short trek located close to Kathmandu, 
making it one of the most accessible trekking routes in Nepal. Known as the 'Valley of Glaciers,' 
the trek offers breathtaking views of Langtang Lirung, Ganesh Himal, Dorje Lakpa, and Shishapangma 
in Tibet. Along the trail, trekkers pass through lush forests, rhododendron valleys, traditional 
Tamang and Sherpa villages, and Buddhist monasteries. The highlight of the trek is the climb to 
Tserko Ri (4,984 m), which provides panoramic sunrise views of the entire Langtang range. This trek 
is perfect for those seeking a Himalayan adventure without venturing too far into remote regions.
fields.itinerary:
Day 01: Arrival in Kathmandu (1,400 m)
Day 02: Drive to Syabrubesi (1,550 m)
Day 03: Trek to Lama Hotel (2,380 m)
Day 04: Trek to Langtang Village (3,430 m)
Day 05: Trek to Kyanjin Gompa (3,870 m)
Day 06: Acclimatization day at Kyanjin Gompa – hike to Tserko Ri (4,984 m)
Day 07: Trek back to Lama Hotel (2,380 m)
Day 08: Trek to Syabrubesi (1,550 m)
Day 09: Drive back to Kathmandu
Day 10: Departure from Kathmandu

fields.title: Manaslu Circuit Trek
fields.description:
The Manaslu Circuit Trek is a remote and less-crowded alternative to the 
Annapurna Circuit, offering an authentic Himalayan experience. This trek circles Mount Manaslu 
(8,163 m), the eighth-highest mountain in the world, and passes through diverse landscapes, 
ranging from subtropical forests and terraced fields to alpine meadows and glacial valleys. 
The cultural experience is equally rich, with Tibetan-influenced villages, ancient monasteries, 
and a unique blend of Buddhist and Hindu traditions. The highlight of the trek is crossing 
Larke La Pass (5,106 m), which offers spectacular panoramic views of Manaslu, Himlung Himal, 
Cheo Himal, and Annapurna II. Due to its remoteness, a special trekking permit is required.
fields.itinerary:
Day 01: Arrival in Kathmandu (1,400 m)
Day 02: Drive to Soti Khola (710 m)
Day 03: Trek to Machha Khola (900 m)
Day 04: Trek to Jagat (1,410 m)
Day 05: Trek to Deng (1,800 m)
Day 06: Trek to Namrung (2,630 m)
Day 07: Trek to Lho Village (3,180 m)
Day 08: Trek to Samagaon (3,530 m) – explore Ribung Monastery
Day 09: Acclimatization day in Samagaon (hike to Manaslu Base Camp or Birendra Lake)
Day 10: Trek to Samdo (3,860 m)
Day 11: Trek to Dharamsala / Larke Phedi (4,460 m)
Day 12: Cross Larke La Pass (5,106 m), trek down to Bimthang (3,720 m)
Day 13: Trek to Tilije (2,300 m)
Day 14: Trek to Dharapani (1,960 m), drive to Besisahar
Day 15: Drive back to Kathmandu
Day 16: Free day in Kathmandu
Day 17: Departure from Kathmandu

fields.title: Upper Dolpo Trek
fields.description:
The Upper Dolpo Trek is one of Nepal’s most remote and culturally fascinating 
treks, taking you into the isolated highlands of Dolpa. This region is strongly influenced by 
Tibetan Buddhism and Bon traditions, with centuries-old monasteries like Shey Gompa. The trek 
passes through arid landscapes, deep gorges, and crystal-clear alpine lakes, including the stunning 
Phoksundo Lake. Trekkers cross multiple high passes over 5,000 m, offering breathtaking views of 
Dhaulagiri and other snowcapped peaks. Due to its remoteness, special permits are required, and 
the trek demands strong physical fitness and endurance.
fields.itinerary:
Day 01: Arrival in Kathmandu (1,400 m)
Day 02: Fly to Nepalgunj (150 m)
Day 03: Fly to Juphal (2,475 m), trek to Dunai
Day 04: Trek to Ankhe
Day 05: Trek to Sulighat
Day 06: Trek to Phoksundo Lake (3,611 m)
Day 07: Acclimatization day at Phoksundo Lake
Day 08: Trek to Phoksundo Khola
Day 09: Trek to Phoksundo Bhanjyang
Day 10: Cross Kang La Pass (5,151 m), trek to Shey Gompa (4,120 m)
Day 11: Exploration day at Shey Gompa
Day 12: Cross Saldang La (4,785 m), trek to Namgung
Day 13: Trek to Saldang (3,620 m)
Day 14: Trek to Yangze Gompa
Day 15: Trek to Sibu
Day 16: Trek to Jeng La Phedi
Day 17: Cross Jeng La Pass (5,030 m), trek to Tokyu Gaon
Day 18: Trek to Dho Tarap (4,090 m)
Day 19: Exploration day at Dho Tarap
Day 20: Trek to Tarap Khola
Day 21: Trek to Khanigaon
Day 22: Trek to Tarakot
Day 23: Trek to Dunai
Day 24: Trek to Juphal
Day 25: Fly to Nepalgunj, then to Kathmandu
Day 26: Free day in Kathmandu
Day 27: Departure from Kathmandu

fields.title: Kanchenjunga Circuit Trek
fields.description:
The Kanchenjunga Circuit Trek is a remote and adventurous trek to the base 
of the world’s third-highest peak, Mt. Kanchenjunga (8,586 m). This trek explores both the north 
and south base camps, offering wild, untouched landscapes and rich biodiversity within Kanchenjunga 
Conservation Area. The journey passes through lush rhododendron forests, alpine meadows, glacial 
valleys, and Tibetan-influenced villages. Trekkers encounter spectacular views of Kanchenjunga, 
Jannu (Kumbhakarna), and several Himalayan giants. Due to its remoteness, this trek requires a 
special permit and is best suited for experienced trekkers looking for solitude and raw adventure.
fields.itinerary:
Day 01: Arrival in Kathmandu (1,400 m)
Day 02: Fly to Bhadrapur, drive to Taplejung
Day 03: Trek to Mitlung (921 m)
Day 04: Trek to Chirwa (1,270 m)
Day 05: Trek to Sekathum (1,660 m)
Day 06: Trek to Amjilosa (2,510 m)
Day 07: Trek to Gyabla (2,730 m)
Day 08: Trek to Ghunsa (3,595 m)
Day 09: Acclimatization day at Ghunsa (hike to Lpasan La viewpoint)
Day 10: Trek to Kambachen (4,050 m)
Day 11: Trek to Lhonak (4,780 m)
Day 12: Trek to Pangpema / Kanchenjunga North Base Camp (5,143 m), return to Lhonak
Day 13: Return trek to Ghunsa
Day 14: Trek to Sele Le
Day 15: Cross Sele La Pass (4,480 m), trek to Cheram
Day 16: Trek to Ramche (4,580 m), excursion to Kanchenjunga South Base Camp (4,600 m)
Day 17: Return to Cheram
Day 18: Trek to Tortong
Day 19: Trek to Yamphudin
Day 20: Trek to Mamankhe
Day 21: Trek to Khebang
Day 22: Drive to Taplejung, then to Bhadrapur
Day 23: Fly to Kathmandu
Day 24: Free day in Kathmandu
Day 25: Departure from Kathmandu

fields.title: Makalu Base Camp Trek
fields.description:
The Makalu Base Camp Trek is a wild and less-traveled trek into the heart of 
Makalu-Barun National Park. It leads to the base camp of Mt. Makalu (8,485 m), the world’s 
fifth-highest mountain. The trek is known for its rugged trails, dramatic landscapes, 
and incredible biodiversity. Trekkers pass through tropical forests, high alpine meadows, 
and remote Sherpa villages before reaching the stark glacial base camp. Unlike other popular routes, 
Makalu offers a true sense of wilderness and solitude. This trek is best for experienced trekkers 
seeking a remote Himalayan adventure.
fields.itinerary:
Day 01: Arrival in Kathmandu (1,400 m)
Day 02: Fly to Tumlingtar, drive to Chichila (1,980 m)
Day 03: Trek to Num (1,560 m)
Day 04: Trek to Seduwa (1,500 m) – entry to Makalu-Barun National Park
Day 05: Trek to Tashigaon (2,100 m)
Day 06: Trek to Khongma Danda (3,560 m)
Day 07: Cross Shipton La Pass (4,170 m), trek to Dobato
Day 08: Trek to Yangle Kharka (3,600 m)
Day 09: Trek to Langmale Kharka (4,410 m)
Day 10: Trek to Makalu Base Camp (5,250 m)
Day 11: Exploration day at Makalu Base Camp
Day 12: Return trek to Yangle Kharka
Day 13: Trek to Khongma Danda
Day 14: Trek to Tashigaon
Day 15: Trek to Num
Day 16: Trek to Chichila, drive to Tumlingtar
Day 17: Fly back to Kathmandu
Day 18: Free day in Kathmandu
Day 19: Departure from Kathmandu

fields.title: Mount Everest
fields.description:
Mount Everest is the world’s highest peak at 8,848 m. Climbing Everest is a 
high-altitude expedition that requires excellent fitness, technical skills, and experience 
with prolonged extreme conditions. The standard route is via the South Col from Nepal, passing 
through the Khumbu Icefall, Western Cwm, and South Col before the summit push.
fields.itinerary:
Day 01: Arrival in Kathmandu (1,400 m)
Day 02: Trek preparation and permit collection
Day 03: Fly to Lukla (2,800 m), trek to Phakding (2,610 m)
Day 04–10: Trek to Everest Base Camp (5,364 m) via Namche Bazaar, Tengboche, Dingboche, Lobuche
Day 11–50: Acclimatization rotations at Base Camp and higher camps
Day 51–60: Summit push via South Col and return to Base Camp
Day 61–63: Trek back to Lukla, fly to Kathmandu
Day 64: Rest and sightseeing in Kathmandu
Day 65–70: Departure from Kathmandu

fields.title: Lhotse
fields.description:
Lhotse is the world’s fourth-highest peak (8,516 m) and shares much of the 
route with Everest until the Lhotse Face. Climbers face steep snow and ice, requiring advanced 
mountaineering experience.
fields.itinerary:
Day 01: Arrival in Kathmandu
Day 02: Fly to Lukla, trek to Namche Bazaar
Day 03–10: Trek to Everest Base Camp
Day 11–50: Acclimatization rotations on Lhotse Face and South Col
Day 51–60: Summit attempt and return to Base Camp
Day 61–63: Trek to Lukla, fly back to Kathmandu
Day 64–65: Rest in Kathmandu
Day 66–70: Departure

fields.title: Nuptse
fields.description:
Nuptse (7,861 m) is a sharp, rocky peak immediately southwest of Everest. 
Climbers face technical rock and ice sections. It is usually climbed by highly experienced mountaineers 
often as part of acclimatization or combined expeditions with Everest.
fields.itinerary:
Day 01: Arrival in Kathmandu
Day 02: Fly to Lukla, trek to Namche Bazaar
Day 03–08: Trek to Base Camp area near Nuptse
Day 09–45: Acclimatization and rotation climbs
Day 46–55: Summit push and return
Day 56–58: Trek to Lukla, fly back to Kathmandu
Day 59–60: Departure

fields.title: Ama Dablam
fields.description:
Ama Dablam is one of Nepal’s most iconic peaks, known for its beautiful 
pyramidal shape. The climb involves steep rock, ice, and mixed terrain. Technical climbing skills 
and high-altitude experience are required.
fields.itinerary:
Day 01: Arrival in Kathmandu
Day 02: Fly to Lukla, trek to Phakding
Day 03–06: Trek to Base Camp (4,600 m) via Namche Bazaar and Tengboche
Day 07–25: Acclimatization rotations and climb via South Ridge
Day 26–30: Summit push and descent
Day 31–33: Trek back to Lukla, fly to Kathmandu
Day 34–35: Rest and departure
